purpose: Architecture-only ω-cycle producing the witness for `nexus kick <topic>` (kick(ω-cycle)) — a CLI subcommand that surfaces the Ω-saturation methodology itself with sub-agent dispatch, multi-CLI backend pluralism, live tree introspection, performance benchmarking, and outside-the-well noise injection wired into the abstraction stage. Deliverable is this witness JSON; code emission is the next cycle.

korean_phrases_preserved: 추상화/상상 단계에서도 노이즈 추가해서 (환각 XXXXX) 창의력 발휘 가능하게끔
trigger.english_paraphrase: Fire ω-cycle on the architecture for `nexus kick`. kick ≡ ω-cycle full equivalence (notation established 2026-04-26). Sub-agent dispatch executor. Multi-CLI backends (claude code cli + codex cli + gemini cli). Composable but NOT 'lens'. Performance bench across backends. Live ASCII tree of registered elements. Outside-the-well noise injection — including at the abstraction/imagination stage — distinguishable from inside-the-well hallucination.

llm_inside_the_well: hallucination = recombining what is already inside
abstraction-stage 'creativity' is post-hoc recombination of frozen prior
nexus_outside_the_well: absorbed primitive feeds the next cycle

trigger.design_constraint_implied: kick(ω-cycle) MUST wire outside-the-well noise sources as first-class injection points — including the abstraction/imagination stage — and MUST keep them distinguishable from inside-the-well recombination at audit time.

notation.established: 2026-04-26 by user; not abbreviation, full equivalence

noise_topology_framing.axis: where does noise enter creative reasoning?
noise_topology_framing.llm_default: inside-the-well — frozen training prior recombined; called 'hallucination' when ungrounded; cannot exceed ceiling of corpus
noise_topology_framing.nexus_target: outside-the-well — fresh empirical bytes (16 bridges + QRNG + absorbed primitives from prior witnesses) inject into the cycle at multiple stages including abstraction
noise_topology_framing.reference_topology: Blowup → Contract → Emerge → Absorb → feeds-next-cycle (user's NEXUS cycle)
noise_topology_framing.audit_requirement: every creative leap must be source-tagged with the outside-noise byte source (bridge name + fetch hash, QRNG seed hash, or absorbed-primitive witness id) so that at audit a reviewer can distinguish outside-noise creativity from inside-the-well hallucination
noise_topology_framing.anti_pattern: 'let the LLM brainstorm more' is inside-the-well recombination; counts as hallucination; explicitly forbidden as primary creativity mechanism

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: no-linear-phases
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: any architecture exposing 'abstraction → discovery → implementation' or any other ordered phase pipeline as primary topology = FAIL
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: grep architecture description for ordered-phase prose; reject if found

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: no-lens-framing
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: any architecture using 'lens' / 'filter' as primary combinator vocabulary = FAIL (cli/blowup/lens/ already squats that namespace; user explicitly rejected the term)
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: grep proposed combinator name; reject if matches lens|filter

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: backend-pluralism-≥3
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: backend layer must natively support claude code cli + codex cli + gemini cli + uniform interface
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: axis_backend_adapter must enumerate ≥3 backends and declare a single Adapter trait

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: perf-bench-comparability
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: performance metrics on comparable axes across backends — wall-time, cost, witness-quality proxies (Tier-1 count, falsifier coverage, dedup ratio)
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: axis_perf_bench must enumerate the 5 axes by name and the surfaces (CLI flag) that emit them

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: kick-core-invariant
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: `kick core` definable independent of topic, backend, composition
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: axis_core decision sentence must not name any specific topic, backend, or combinator instance

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: tree-introspection-live
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: `nexus kick tree` reflects actually-registered elements at runtime (registry file or directory scan), not hardcoded
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: axis_registry must declare on-disk registry path + scan mechanism; tree_render_example must annotate which leaves are scanned vs config-declared

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: architecture-only
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: this cycle emits witness JSON. NO code
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: files_touched.created should list only this JSON + the kick/ dir; no .hexa/.sh/.py code emission

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: sub-agent-dispatch
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: executor is a sub-agent (separate context). In-process execution = FAIL
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: axis_dispatch decision must route through tool/subagent_dispatch.hexa or analogous spawn mechanism with separate session id

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: honesty-triad
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: no fabrication. Any cited file/function/flag must be greppable. raw 73

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: append-only
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: don't propose deleting existing nexus CLI subcommands. raw 77
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: axis decisions must add cmd_kick alongside existing cmd_drill/cmd_omega/cmd_atlas/cmd_roadmap/cmd_hexa_sim — never replace

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: outside-noise-only
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: noise sources must be outside-the-well (real APIs, QRNG, fresh empirical signal, absorbed primitives from prior cycles). 'let LLM brainstorm more' = inside-the-well = FAIL
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: axis_noise_topology must enumerate sources from {16 bridges, ANU QRNG, absorbed-primitive registry, user-pinned external corpus}; LLM-only sources rejected

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: noise-at-abstraction
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: outside-noise injection points must include the abstraction/imagination stage, not only final candidate enumeration
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: axis_abstraction_noise must declare ≥1 mechanism that injects outside-noise BEFORE candidate enumeration completes

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: noise-distinguishability
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: at audit time, outside-noise injections must be distinguishable from inside-the-well recombination — source-tagged, hash-anchored, citation-bearing
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: every noise injection point must declare {source_id, byte_hash | seed_hash | witness_id, ts} schema in axis_noise_topology

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: NEXUS-cycle-respect
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: noise flow respects Blowup → Contract → Emerge → Absorb → feeds-next-cycle
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: axis_topology must map kick stages onto the four NEXUS stages (under whatever names) for the noise-handling path; absorbed-primitive output must feed the next-cycle registry

candidate_source_families_consulted.extracted: cmd_drill / cmd_omega / cmd_atlas / cmd_roadmap / cmd_hexa_sim / _hexa_sim_bridge_dispatch sub-dispatch shape; help-printer + sub-token switch idiom; `bridge` sub does its own _hexa_sim_bridge_dispatch fanout

candidate_source_families_consulted.name: existing witness JSON convention

candidate_source_families_consulted.name: progressive deepening methodology
candidate_source_families_consulted.extracted: 8-step ω-cycle workflow + 5-pattern progressive deepening + L1→L4 R1 case study columns (Level / Framework / Obstruction / Sanity check)

candidate_source_families_consulted.name: NEXUS noise topology

candidate_source_families_consulted.name: abstraction_ceiling.md drill ladder + raw 48 multi-axis
candidate_source_families_consulted.extracted: stratum vocabulary L1-L_ω confirmed (smash/free/absolute/drill/surge/...omega). raw 48 multi-axis-orthogonal-witness mandates K≥3 orthogonal axes with pairwise correlation < 0.7 — this IS the combinator vocabulary the user wants (axes, not lenses)

axes_surfaced.name: kick core invariant kernel
options_considered: A. core = 'sub-agent spawner' (rejected: backend-specific, fails F5)
B. core = 'witness JSON emitter' (rejected: output-only, no methodology)
C. core = 'falsifier-pre-registration → axis-fanout → tier-1-promotion → witness-emit pipeline' (chosen: methodology-grounded, topic/backend/composition independent)
D. core = 'state/blockers/<slug>.hexa autofire wrapper' (rejected: collapses kick into raw 45 special case; fails F5 because composition-dependent)
axes_surfaced.decision: C — kick core ≡ five invariant operations: (1) topic intake, (2) pre-register falsifiers, (3) fan out heterogeneous candidate axes, (4) gate Tier-1 promotion via falsifier-pass-pattern, (5) emit witness JSON. None of the five name a topic, a backend, or a combinator instance.
axes_surfaced.rationale: Mirrors progressive_deepening_methodology.md 8-step workflow steps 1-3+4+8. Topic-agnostic (any <topic> string). Backend-agnostic (any executor that can read+write text). Composition-agnostic (combinator lives in axis-3 fanout, not in core).

falsifier_verdicts.F5: PASS — five ops cite no topic/backend/combinator
falsifier_verdicts.F11: PASS — falsifier pre-registration is the inside/outside gate

axes_surfaced.name: combinator vocabulary (replacing 'lens')
options_considered: A. 'lens' (REJECTED by F2 + user; cli/blowup/lens/ namespace squat)
B. 'filter' (REJECTED by F2 — same family as lens)
C. 'stratum × axis' (chosen: stratum from abstraction_ceiling.md L1-L_ω ladder; axis from raw 48 multi-axis-orthogonal-witness mandate of K≥3 orthogonal axes)
D. 'lattice node' (rejected: too abstract, no methodology grounding)
E. 'mode' (rejected: collides with cmd_omega/cmd_drill mode flags)
axes_surfaced.decision: C — stratum × axis. A `kick` instance is `kick(topic, stratum=L_n, axes=[a1..aK])` where stratum picks the ω-ladder rung (smash/drill/surge/omega/...) and axes are the K≥3 orthogonal measurement axes mandated by raw 48. Composition is the cartesian {stratum} × {axis-set} product; combinatorial extensibility comes from registering new strata or new axes independently.
axes_surfaced.rationale: Both vocabulary halves are derived from existing methodology, not invented. Stratum is the existing drill→omega ladder (verified in abstraction_ceiling.md §1 table). Axis is the raw 48 enforced concept ('K≥3 orthogonal axes... pairwise correlation < 0.7') — the lint already exists at tool/design_strategy_lint.hexa#multi-axis-orthogonal. F2 passes because neither 'lens' nor 'filter' appears.

falsifier_verdicts.F5: PASS — both are independent of topic/backend

axes_surfaced.name: registry for live tree introspection
options_considered: A. hardcoded list inside cmd_kick_tree (REJECTED by F6)
B. directory-scan of state/kick/registry/{strata,axes,backends,noise-sources}/*.hexa (chosen — mirrors state/blockers/ pattern)
C. single TSV at state/kick_registry.tsv (rejected: less extensible per element type, harder to ship per-element metadata)
D. JSONL append-only (rejected: introspection requires per-element file overwrite for state changes; JSONL is for events not registry)
axes_surfaced.decision: B — four parallel registry directories under state/kick/registry/: strata/, axes/, backends/, noise_sources/. Each directory contains one indented key/value DSL .hexa file per element (same shape as state/blockers/<slug>.hexa). nexus kick tree scans the four directories at runtime and renders. Adding a new backend = drop a new .hexa under backends/. Removing = move out (raw 77 append-only at the registry level: registry never auto-deletes).
axes_surfaced.rationale: Directly reuses raw 45 directory-scan precedent (state/blockers/ + tool/blocker_autofire.hexa walks). Per-element granularity supports per-element metadata (cost-per-1k-tok for backends, sample rate for noise sources, axis correlation thresholds for axes). Per-file SHA pinning available later (mirrors state/bridge_sha256.tsv pattern from bridge_health R1).

axes_surfaced.name: uniform CLI backend adapter
options_considered: A. one wrapper script per backend, no shared trait (rejected: violates F3 'uniform interface')
B. shared Adapter trait with {prepare_prompt, spawn, stream, capture_witness, teardown, cost_estimate} (chosen)
C. provider-shape from roadmaps/hive.json (claude_code.hexa pattern) extended (chosen as concrete shape — already specifies stream-json + latency/cost log + account auto-fallback)
axes_surfaced.decision: Combine B+C. Adapter trait surface (next-cycle code emission target): `prepare_prompt(topic, falsifiers, axes) -> str`, `spawn(prompt, opts) -> child_handle`, `stream(child_handle) -> iterator<event>`, `capture_witness(child_handle, expected_path) -> witness_json | err`, `teardown(child_handle) -> void`, `cost_estimate(prompt) -> {tokens_in, tokens_out_estimate, usd_estimate}`. Concrete adapters: backends/claude_code.hexa, backends/codex_cli.hexa, backends/gemini_cli.hexa. Each adapter declares its sentinel format (raw 80) and its err schema (raw 66 reason+fix trailer).
axes_surfaced.rationale: F3 requires ≥3 backends — claude_code + codex_cli + gemini_cli is the named minimum. roadmaps/hive.json already specifies claude_code.hexa shape (verified at line 152). Adapter trait keeps F4 perf-bench-comparability tractable: same six methods → same five metrics across all backends.

falsifier_verdicts.F3: PASS — three named backends + uniform 6-method trait
falsifier_verdicts.F4: PASS — cost_estimate + stream timing + capture_witness give wall-time/cost/quality on the same surface

axes_surfaced.name: performance benchmark surface
options_considered: A. inline metrics in witness JSON only (rejected: no cross-witness comparability surface)
B. nexus kick bench <topic> --backends a,b,c (chosen — explicit bench subcommand)
C. nexus kick tree --bench (chosen as secondary surface — overlay last-bench numbers on tree)
D. external prom/grafana exporter (rejected: out-of-scope this cycle)
axes_surfaced.decision: B+C. Five comparable axes captured per-backend per-topic: (1) wall_time_ms, (2) cost_usd_estimate (token proxy when USD unavailable), (3) tier_1_count (witness-quality proxy 1), (4) falsifier_coverage_pct (witness-quality proxy 2 — % of pre-registered falsifiers with explicit verdict_check populated), (5) dedup_ratio_vs_prior (1.0 - jaccard(this_witness.tier_1_slugs, last_witness_for_same_topic.tier_1_slugs); high = novel). Bench results land at state/kick/bench/<date>_<topic_slug>_<backend>.jsonl.
axes_surfaced.rationale: F4 explicit list satisfied. Each axis is computable from witness JSON + adapter cost_estimate. dedup_ratio_vs_prior is the inside-the-well-detector — a backend that keeps re-promoting the same Tier-1s on repeated kicks is doing recombination not exploration.

falsifier_verdicts.F4: PASS — five axes named, all comparable across backends

axes_surfaced.name: sub-agent dispatch mechanics
options_considered: A. in-process spawn (REJECTED by F8)
B. tool/subagent_dispatch.hexa direct invocation (chosen — already exists at /Users/ghost/core/hive/tool/subagent_dispatch.hexa, proven by raw 45 blocker_autofire wire)
C. blocker_autofire-style state/blockers/<slug>.hexa registration (chosen for SUSTAINED kicks — kick(ω-cycle) that should re-fire until convergence witness lands)
D. directive-emission for external runner (rejected: loses the parent SID propagation contract from raw 44)
axes_surfaced.decision: B for one-shot `nexus kick <topic>`; C for `nexus kick <topic> --until-converged` which writes state/blockers/kick_<slug>.hexa with fire-cmd routing back through subagent_dispatch.hexa. Both modes hard-require parent-sid via $HIVE_PARENT_SID env or --parent-sid flag (raw 44). Backend adapter (axis_backend_adapter) is what subagent_dispatch.hexa actually spawns — the adapter is the inner shell of the sub-agent.
axes_surfaced.rationale: F8 satisfied via separate-context spawn. Reuses verified subagent_dispatch.hexa (greppable at /Users/ghost/core/hive/tool/subagent_dispatch.hexa lines 1-516 verified). raw 44 parent SID re-tagging is already shipped — kick inherits SSE-event-flow-back-to-parent for free.

falsifier_verdicts.F10: PASS — additive, does not delete existing dispatch tooling

axes_surfaced.name: witness JSON landing path
options_considered: A. design/kick/<date>_<topic_slug>_omega_cycle.json (chosen — matches design/hexa_sim/, design/cross_engine/, design/roadmap_engine/ namespace convention)

B. state/kick/witnesses/<...>.json (rejected: state/ is .gitignored; witnesses are part of historical record per raw 77 append-only ledger and should be git-tracked like sibling design/<engine>/ dirs)
C. flat design/<...>.json (rejected: namespace pollution)
axes_surfaced.decision: A. Path schema: design/kick/<YYYY-MM-DD>_<topic_slug>_omega_cycle.json. topic_slug = lowercase + dashes from <topic>. Multiple kicks on same topic same day suffix _v2/_v3.
axes_surfaced.rationale: Design dir is not in .gitignore (verified — state/* is, design/* is not). Sibling namespaces (cross_engine/ roadmap_engine/ hexa_sim/) use this exact pattern. Append-only ledger (raw 77) is satisfied: new file per kick, never overwrite.

axes_surfaced.name: non-linear topology — explicit reject of linear pipeline
options_considered: A. linear abstraction → discovery → implementation (REJECTED by F1)
B. axis-parallel + obstruction-driven progressive deepening (chosen — directly from progressive_deepening_methodology.md 5-pattern)
C. DAG with per-node retry (rejected: degenerates to linear in practice unless obstruction handling is explicit)
axes_surfaced.decision: B. Topology is: K≥3 axes execute in parallel under one stratum. Each axis emits per-axis verdict. On any axis OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED: 5-pattern (identify → document → structural reframe → sanity check → cumulative learning) fires for THAT axis only; other axes continue. Cross-axis evidence localization (3-pillar) collapses obstructions to a common ingredient when ≥2 axes hit related failure. NEXUS noise cycle maps onto axis lifecycle: Blowup = candidate fanout under axis, Contract = falsifier discrimination, Emerge = Tier-1 promotion, Absorb = cumulative-learning entry into next-cycle absorbed-primitive registry.
axes_surfaced.rationale: F1 satisfied: no ordered phase pipeline; axes are simultaneous. F14 satisfied: explicit Blowup/Contract/Emerge/Absorb mapping with named lifecycle stage per axis. progressive_deepening_methodology.md is the verified source (file read).

falsifier_verdicts.F14: PASS — Blowup→Contract→Emerge→Absorb mapped onto axis lifecycle

axes_surfaced.name: ASCII tree render shape
options_considered: A. flat list (rejected: doesn't communicate composition)
B. 4-bucket tree: core / strata / axes / backends / noise-sources (chosen)
C. graphviz dot output (rejected: ASCII required per user spec)
axes_surfaced.decision: B (5 buckets when noise-sources counted separately). Top-level: kick(ω-cycle). Children: core (5 invariant ops), strata/ (registered ladder rungs), axes/ (registered orthogonal axes), backends/ (registered CLI adapters), noise_sources/ (registered outside-noise feeds). Each leaf shows registry-file-of-record + last-bench-summary when --bench.
axes_surfaced.rationale: Mirrors how `nexus hexa-sim` self-documents via _hexa_sim_help() but extended with live directory scan (F6).

axes_surfaced.name: outside-the-well noise injection topology
options_considered: A. no explicit noise injection (REJECTED by F11+F12 — collapses to inside-the-well)
B. noise only at candidate enumeration (REJECTED by F12 — abstraction stage uncovered)
C. noise at three stages with source-tagging (chosen)
D. random LLM temperature increase (REJECTED — temperature samples the same trained prior, still inside the well)
axes_surfaced.decision: C. Three injection points, each with {source_id, byte_hash | seed_hash | witness_id, ts} envelope: (i) ABSTRACTION-stage seed: when topic is parsed, the topic prompt is augmented with K_seed empirical bytes drawn from a randomly-selected subset of the 16 bridges (codata, oeis, gw, horizons, arxiv, cmb, nanograv, simbad, icecube, nist_atomic, wikipedia, openalex, gaia, lhc, pubchem, uniprot) plus one ANU QRNG seed (verified path /Users/ghost/core/nexus/sim_bridge/anu_time/). (ii) AXIS-fanout stage: each axis is paired with at least one bridge source whose latest fetch hash is recorded as an input. (iii) NEXT-cycle absorption: every Tier-1 promoted in this cycle becomes an entry in state/kick/registry/noise_sources/absorbed_<slug>.hexa for use as primitive in subsequent kicks. NEXUS Blowup→Contract→Emerge→Absorb mapping: Blowup = noise-augmented candidate fanout (i+ii), Contract = falsifier-gate discrimination, Emerge = Tier-1 promotion, Absorb = (iii) write-to-noise_sources registry feeding the next kick.
axes_surfaced.rationale: F11 satisfied: all three injections come from outside-the-well sources (real-API bridges + QRNG + prior-witness bytes). F13 satisfied: every injection envelope is hash-anchored — at audit, a reviewer can trace any creative leap to its byte-source. F14 satisfied: NEXUS four-stage cycle is the explicit topology, with absorbed-primitive feedback wired.

falsifier_verdicts.F11: PASS — bridges + QRNG + absorbed primitives, no LLM-only

axes_surfaced.name: outside-noise at the abstraction/imagination stage
options_considered: A. defer noise until post-abstraction (REJECTED by F12)
B. random primitive sampling from absorbed-primitive registry of prior witnesses (chosen — sub-mechanism α)
C. QRNG-seeded axis pre-selection (chosen — sub-mechanism β)
D. empirical-bridge-driven domain reframe (chosen — sub-mechanism γ)
E. LLM-only brainstorm with high temperature (REJECTED by F11 — inside-the-well)
axes_surfaced.decision: Triple mechanism α+β+γ at the abstraction stage, all required, all source-tagged. (α) Absorbed-primitive sampling: scan state/kick/registry/noise_sources/absorbed_*.hexa, draw N primitives uniformly with QRNG-seeded random; primitives are previously-witnessed Tier-1s (cross-cycle memory). (β) QRNG-seeded axis pre-selection: ANU byte stream selects which K of the registered axes fire for this kick — randomization de-anchors from the user-prompt's implicit axis affordance, breaking inside-the-well 'always pick the obvious axis' bias. (γ) Empirical-bridge-driven reframe: one bridge is fetched at abstraction-time (e.g. arxiv recent abstract, oeis adjacent sequence, gaia stellar params) and its content is injected as REFRAME-CONTEXT in the prompt to the executor sub-agent. Each of α/β/γ logs its source envelope to the witness JSON's `noise_injections.abstraction_stage` array.
axes_surfaced.rationale: F12 satisfied: all three mechanisms inject at abstraction-stage (BEFORE any axis fans out candidates). F13 satisfied: α envelope = {witness_id of source absorbed primitive}, β envelope = {anu_seed_hash, byte_offset}, γ envelope = {bridge_name, fetch_hash, fetched_ts}. None of the three relies on LLM-internal recombination. The 'random LLM temperature' alternative is explicitly rejected because temperature samples the trained prior — same well.

falsifier_verdicts.F12: PASS — abstraction stage covered by all three
falsifier_verdicts.F13: PASS — each mechanism has explicit envelope schema

tier_1_promotions.rationale: Five invariant operations (intake → falsifier-pre-register → axis-fanout → tier1-gate → witness-emit) are the ONLY surface that must be present in every kick(ω-cycle), regardless of topic, backend, or composition. This is the load-bearing claim of the architecture.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: passes F5 (no topic/backend/combinator naming), F7 (architecture-only), F11 (falsifier-pre-register IS the inside/outside gate)

tier_1_promotions.rationale: Stratum × axis vocabulary is derived (not invented) from abstraction_ceiling.md drill ladder + raw 48 multi-axis-orthogonal mandate. Replaces 'lens' and avoids the cli/blowup/lens/ namespace squat. Combinatorial: register a new stratum or new axis without touching either of the others.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: passes F2 (vocabulary is stratum/axis), F5 (each is independent of topic+backend), F10 (additive — both registries support add-only)

tier_1_promotions.rationale: Triple-mechanism abstraction-stage outside-noise (α absorbed-primitive sampling + β QRNG axis pre-selection + γ bridge-driven reframe) is the architectural answer to the user's '추상화/상상 단계에서도 노이즈 추가해서 (환각 XXXXX) 창의력 발휘' directive. Each mechanism is hash-anchored so audit-time inside/outside-the-well distinction is mechanical, not subjective.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: passes F11 (all three outside-the-well), F12 (all three at abstraction stage), F13 (hash envelopes), F14 (NEXUS Blowup→Contract→Emerge→Absorb explicit)

tier_1_promotions.rationale: Six-method Adapter trait (prepare_prompt / spawn / stream / capture_witness / teardown / cost_estimate) gives one surface across claude_code + codex_cli + gemini_cli, and that single surface is what makes the five perf-bench axes (wall_time / cost_usd / tier1_count / falsifier_coverage / dedup_ratio_vs_prior) cross-backend comparable. Without the trait, F3 and F4 cannot both pass.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: passes F3 (≥3 backends + uniform interface), F4 (5 named comparable axes), F8 (each adapter spawned via subagent_dispatch.hexa)

obstructions.step_1_identification: codex cli has zero prior references in /Users/ghost/core/nexus/ and /Users/ghost/core/hive/ (grep verified — only claude-code and gemini_cli appear in roadmaps/hive.json)
obstructions.step_2_documentation: OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED: codex_cli adapter is fully greenfield. Cannot copy a known-shape from prior wrappers. Risk: invented invocation contract may not match actual codex CLI surface area.
obstructions.step_3_structural_reframe: Reframe from 'mirror an existing wrapper' to 'derive contract from claude_code.hexa shape (verified at roadmaps/hive.json:152) which uses -p headless mode + stream-json — assume codex cli has analogous flags; write adapter against probed reality during code-emission cycle, not architecture cycle'
obstructions.step_4_sanity_check: Architecture treats codex_cli as a slot in the registry; the slot may remain unfilled at code-emission until the actual codex CLI shape is probed. F3 still passes architecturally because the registry slot exists; runtime registration is what fills it.
obstructions.step_5_cumulative_learning: Backend adapter design must keep the registry-driven model so that backends with unknown shape can be added via registry entry without changing core. This reinforces axis_registry decision (state/kick/registry/backends/<name>.hexa as plug-in).

obstructions.step_1_identification: ANU bridge rate limit verified in sim_bridge/anu_time/README.md: '1 req/min, 64 bytes/response → natural rate ~1 byte/s'. urandom fallback exists.
obstructions.step_2_documentation: OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED: high-frequency kicks would exhaust ANU within seconds, falling back to urandom — which is technically still outside-the-well (kernel entropy pool), but no longer quantum-witnessed.
obstructions.step_3_structural_reframe: Re-cast β as 'QRNG-PREFERRED-WITH-URANDOM-FALLBACK' and require the seed_hash envelope to record `source ∈ {anu, urandom}` so audit can detect when fallback dominated.
obstructions.step_4_sanity_check: anu_time runner already records this field per tick (verified in README.md schema: `{tau, wall_t_ns, byte_dt_ns, byte, source, ...}`). Inheriting that schema means no new mechanism needed.
obstructions.step_5_cumulative_learning: Outside-noise sources will routinely have rate limits. Architecture must record source-degradation transparently rather than silently fall through. Generalizes to bridges: bridge_health.sh OFFLINE-FALLBACK semantic (verified at /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/bridge_health.sh:142+) is the precedent — kick noise injections inherit the same OFFLINE-FALLBACK + degraded-but-witnessed posture.

tree_render_example:
kick(ω-cycle)
├── core/
│   ├── intake (topic-string)
│   ├── falsifier_preregister (state/kick/registry/falsifiers_default.hexa + topic-specific)
│   ├── axis_fanout (parallel)
│   ├── tier1_gate (≤4 promotion + falsifier-pass-pattern required)
│   └── witness_emit (design/kick/<date>_<slug>_omega_cycle.json)
├── strata/                              [scan: state/kick/registry/strata/*.hexa]
│   ├── smash       (L1, atomic single-phase)
│   ├── drill       (L2, iterate × rounds)
│   ├── drill_batch (L3, fan-out seeds)
│   ├── surge       (L4, super-orch, placeholder)
│   └── omega       (L_ω, formal+physical ceiling)
├── axes/                                [scan: state/kick/registry/axes/*.hexa]
│   ├── design-strategy        (raw 47 + 48)
│   ├── falsifier-coverage     (raw 71 + 73)
│   ├── perf-comparability     (this cycle, F4-driven)
│   ├── noise-distinguishability (this cycle, F13-driven)
│   └── (≥3 required per raw 48; pairwise correlation < 0.7 enforced by tool/design_strategy_lint.hexa#multi-axis-orthogonal)
├── backends/                            [scan: state/kick/registry/backends/*.hexa]
│   ├── claude_code  (CLI: claude -p, stream-json; cost: per-token usd)
│   ├── codex_cli    (CLI: TBD; greenfield slot — see obs_1)
│   └── gemini_cli   (CLI: gemini, 200K-2M ctx)
└── noise_sources/                       [scan: state/kick/registry/noise_sources/*.hexa]
    ├── bridges/                         [16 entries from tool/bridge_health.sh BRIDGES list]
    │   ├── codata    oeis        gw         horizons    arxiv
    │   ├── cmb       nanograv    simbad     icecube     nist_atomic
    │   └── wikipedia openalex    gaia       lhc         pubchem    uniprot
    ├── qrng/
    │   └── anu_time (sim_bridge/anu_time/, urandom-fallback witnessed per OBSTRUCTION obs_2)
    └── absorbed/                        [grows monotonically per cycle — Tier-1s of prior kicks]
        └── (initially empty; populated as Tier-1 promotions accumulate across cycles)

files_to_create.edit_type: additive — new fn cmd_kick(a: [str]) and dispatch entry alongside cmd_drill/cmd_omega/cmd_atlas/cmd_roadmap/cmd_hexa_sim

fn _kick_tree() -> void          // scans state/kick/registry/{strata,axes,backends,noise_sources}/ and renders ASCII
fn _kick_bench(a: [str]) -> void // wraps cmd_kick run loops over backends, emits state/kick/bench/<...>.jsonl

files_to_create.edit_type: create — one indented key/value DSL file per stratum, fields: slug, level (L1..L_ω), description, dispatch-fn (cmd_drill | cmd_omega | ...)
create — fields: slug, description, lint-fn (tool/design_strategy_lint.hexa#multi-axis-orthogonal etc.), correlation-budget (default 0.7)
create — fields: slug, cli-binary, prepare-prompt-fn, spawn-fn, stream-fn, capture-witness-fn, teardown-fn, cost-estimate-fn, sentinel-format (raw 80), err-trailer-format (raw 66)
create — 16 bridge entries + 1 QRNG entry initially; absorbed_*.hexa added by witness_emit after each cycle
create — wraps tool/subagent_dispatch.hexa for single-shot kicks. Resolves backend from registry, builds prompt with α/β/γ noise injections, spawns sub-agent, captures witness JSON.
create — directory-scan implementation of _kick_tree (standalone for test + reuse from cmd_kick)
create — runs the same topic across registered backends, captures the 5 perf axes, writes JSONL
create — for `nexus kick <topic> --until-converged` mode; standard raw 45 blocker entry with fire-cmd routed through tool/subagent_dispatch.hexa + dispatch-mode subagent

next_cycle_handoff.registry_dsl_example:
# state/kick/registry/backends/claude_code.hexa
backend 1 active "claude code cli — Anthropic CLI in headless -p mode"
  slug claude_code
  cli-binary claude
  invocation -p --output-format stream-json
  prepare-prompt-fn _kick_prompt_claude_code
  spawn-fn _kick_spawn_claude_code
  stream-fn _kick_stream_claude_code
  capture-witness-fn _kick_capture_witness_claude_code
  teardown-fn _kick_teardown_claude_code
  cost-estimate-fn _kick_cost_claude_code
  sentinel-format __KICK_RESULT__ <PASS|FAIL> witness=<path> tier1=<N> falsifier_pass=<N>
  err-trailer reason=<slug> fix=<directive>
